qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Angular service worker dataGroups can replay cached authenticated responses across authorization contexts
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: The finding is a service-worker cache isolation flaw caused by forced `ignoreVary: true` in `packages/service-worker/config/src/generator.ts` and cache lookup behavior in `packages/service-worker/worker/src/data.ts`. GHSA-g93w-mfhg-p222 instead fixes missing sanitization of translated i18n attribute bindings and related sensitive sinks in `packages/core/src/render3/i18n/i18n_parse.ts` and compiler sink definitions. Same repository, different component and root cause.

target_advisory_judgement.reason: No qualifying finding identifies the advisory-described vulnerable code or an equivalent root cause. GHSA-g93w-mfhg-p222 is an Angular i18n attribute-binding sanitization/XSS issue in core/compiler, while the session finding is a service-worker cache replay bug.
evidence: The advisory summary and affected-package list target i18n attribute-binding XSS in `@angular/core` and `@angular/compiler`.
Angular PR #67541 patches `packages/core/src/render3/i18n/i18n_parse.ts` so translated sensitive attributes such as `href` interpolations are sanitized.
Angular PR #67561 extends the fix set to translated form attributes and compiler trusted-type sinks such as `iframe src`.

The only final finding points to `packages/service-worker/config/src/generator.ts`, `packages/service-worker/config/src/in.ts`, and `packages/service-worker/worker/src/data.ts`, where `cacheQueryOptions.ignoreVary = true` causes cached-response replay across authorization contexts.
